name: Ombria del Benicadell Loop
name_original: Gravel tour por l'Ombria del Benicadell — Circular desde Atzeneta d'Albaida
description: A difficult 21.7-mile (34.9 km) gravel tour from Atzeneta d'Albaida, climbing 2654 feet (809 metres) through pine forests with valley views.
region_intro: Hiking around Atzeneta D'Albaida reveals a landscape characterized by rolling hills, pine forests, and historical landmarks. The region features varied elevations, with routes ascending to viewpoints and passing through natural areas. Trails often lead through Mediterranean scrubland and offer glimpses of local architecture and historical sites. Best hiking trails around Atzeneta D'Albaida The most popular hiking route is Benicadell – Benicadell Ice House loop from Port d'Albaida, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Benicadell mountain and historical ice houses. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Carrícola Castle loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views of Carrícola Castle and the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Mirador Font Freda loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Atzeneta D'Albaida is defined by hilly terrain, pine forests, and historical sites. The network offers options for moderate to more challenging hikes. The routes in Atzeneta D'Albaida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 2,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Atzeneta D'Albaida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails available around Atzeneta D'Albaida, offering a variety of experiences through the region's diverse landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The best time to hike in Atzeneta D'Albaida is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). The weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: While most routes in Atzeneta D'Albaida are rated as moderate, there are options that are shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for those new to hiking. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17298517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carrícola Castle loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) path with manageable elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Hiking around Atzeneta D'Albaida features rolling hills, pine forests, and Mediterranean scrubland. Trails often include varied elevations, leading to viewpoints and passing through natural areas and historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida are circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3959189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benicadell – Benicadell Ice House loop from Port d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Font Freda loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places can I explore near the hiking trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Along and near the hiking trails, you can discover several points of interest. These include historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2546615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres&lt;/a&gt; and natural landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beniarrés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural swimming spots close to the hiking routes?
A: Yes, a notable natural swimming spot in the vicinity is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;. This area offers refreshing waters, perfect for a dip after a hike, and is a popular local attraction.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida are suitable for families, especially those looking for moderate walks. Routes with less elevation and shorter distances, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17298517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carrícola Castle loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt;, can be enjoyable for children, offering scenic views and historical interest.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in natural parks or areas with livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads in and around Atzeneta D'Albaida, particularly those starting from towns like Port d'Albaida or Atzeneta d'Albaida itself, offer designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The duration of hikes in Atzeneta D'Albaida varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. Most moderate trails range from 2 to 5 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3959189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benicadell – Benicadell Ice House loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours 45 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. Reviewers often praise the scenic views of the Benicadell mountain, the historical ice houses, and the diverse landscapes that combine pine forests with Mediterranean scrubland.
tips: Entertaining gravel track with amazing views! Excellent surface to roll without worries and be able to enjoy the landscape and Benicadell, which are wonderful!

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult touring bicycle ride by komoot, and research describes it as a 'moderate mountain bike ride' requiring 'good fitness'. While much of it is suitable for all skill levels in terms of riding technique, be aware that some sections may demand advanced riding skills, falling into the S2 category of the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this gravel tour in Atzeneta d'Albaida?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in Atzeneta d'Albaida. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find public parking within the town. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a convenient spot near the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Benicadell gravel tour?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows a track along the northern face of the Sierra de Benicadell. It's generally gravel-friendly, but expect varied surfaces. The northern slope is known for being cold and humid, passing through beautiful pine forests. While mostly manageable, some sections may require advanced riding skills due to their technical nature.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant part of the route follows the L'Ombría del Benicadell Trail. This section offers incredible views over the extensive valleys of Albaida and Comtat. The 'high mountain atmosphere' created by clouds clinging to the walls of the Sierra de Benicadell is a notable natural feature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the northern slope's tendency to be cold and humid with frequent clouds, the warmer months from late spring to early autumn (May to October) are generally the most pleasant for this tour. However, always check the local weather forecast before you go, as conditions can change rapidly in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Does this gravel tour intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes and intersects with several official trails. Notably, significant portions follow the Camino del Alba (36%) and the Pista Forestal de l'Ombria de Benicadell (approximately 44% combined). Other smaller sections include El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camí de la Font del Melero, and Camí del Corral de Poldo.

faqs.question: Is the Ombria del Benicadell Protected Landscape part of this tour, and are there any permits or fees required?
faqs.answer: Yes, this gravel tour takes you through the Ombria del Benicadell Protected Landscape. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on the established tracks within the protected area. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this gravel tour suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating by komoot and sections requiring 'advanced riding skills' (S2 category), this route is not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good fitness and some experience with off-road cycling. There are likely more suitable, less challenging routes in the wider Valencia region for those new to gravel biking.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, it's essential to bring sufficient water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), and appropriate cycling gear. Layers of clothing are recommended due to the humid and potentially cooler conditions on the northern slope. A helmet is, of course, mandatory, and a GPS device with the route loaded is highly advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the route?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in Atzeneta d'Albaida, a town where you can find local cafes and restaurants to refuel before or after your ride. There are no specific facilities directly on the trail itself, so plan accordingly for food and drink during the ride.

faqs.question: Is the 'Gravel tour through the shadow of Benicadell' dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular gravel tour are not explicitly stated. As a general rule in protected landscapes, dogs are often permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage or official park websites for the most current rules before bringing your dog.
